title: spiraea crenata subsp. crenata
intro: Spiraea crenata subsp. crenata, also known as Japanese Spirea or Crenate Spirea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective effects. Spiraea crenata subsp. crenata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine and folk remedies.
